title: Flawed In-Order Node Removal in Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree and an array of node IDs to remove, implement a function to remove nodes from the tree in-order (left->root->right) and return the modified tree.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as a Node class with value, left, and right children.
statement.outputFormat: The modified binary tree with nodes removed in-order.
statement.notes: Nodes to remove are given as an array of IDs, where each ID corresponds to a node in the tree.

hints.text: In-order node removal requires a recursive DFS approach to traverse the tree.

editorial.title: Correct In-Order Node Removal Approach
editorial.intuition: In-order node removal involves recursively finding the node to be removed and its children in the tree, then connecting the remaining children to the parent node.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve traversing the tree, finding each node to be removed, and reconnecting the children.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a recursive DFS approach to traverse the tree, finding and removing nodes in-order, and reconnecting the remaining children.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness is ensured by ensuring that the tree remains a valid binary tree after node removal.
editorial.dryRun:
Example dry run: Remove node 5 from binary tree: 4
3 5
2
\     6

functionSignature.python: def removeNodes(self, root, toRemove: list[int]): TreeNode:
